title: Quiz: Rotation Gates
description: Test your understanding of Rx, Ry, and Rz rotation gates

questions.question: What is Rx(π) equal to (up to global phase)?
questions.explanation: Rx(π) = -iX. The π rotation around the X-axis is equivalent to the Pauli X gate up to a global phase of -i.

questions.question: Which rotation gate has a purely real matrix (no imaginary entries)?
questions.explanation: Ry(θ) = [[cos(θ/2), -sin(θ/2)], [sin(θ/2), cos(θ/2)]] contains only real entries.

questions.question: Rotations around different axes always commute: Rx(θ)Ry(φ) = Ry(φ)Rx(θ).
questions.explanation: False! Rotations around different axes do NOT commute in general. Only rotations around the same axis commute.

questions.question: In the ZYZ decomposition U = e^(iα) Rz(β) Ry(γ) Rz(δ), what does γ control?

options.text: The polar angle θ on the Bloch sphere
The azimuthal angle φ on the Bloch sphere

questions.explanation: The Ry(γ) rotation in the middle controls how far the state moves from the poles (the polar angle).

questions.question: What is the diagonal form of Rz(θ)?
questions.explanation: Rz(θ) = [[e^(-iθ/2), 0], [0, e^(iθ/2)]]. Note this differs from P(θ) by a global phase.

questions.question: To create any pure single-qubit state from |0⟩, the minimum number of rotation gates needed is:
questions.explanation: Two rotations suffice: Rz(φ)Ry(θ)|0⟩ can create any state |ψ⟩ = cos(θ/2)|0⟩ + e^(iφ)sin(θ/2)|1⟩.
